CREATE TABLE samples (id INT, x FLOAT, y FLOAT);
SELECT covar_samp(y, x) AS covariance FROM samples;
CREATE TABLE more_samples (id INT, x FLOAT, y FLOAT);
SELECT covar_samp(y, x) AS covariance FROM (SELECT x, y FROM samples UNION ALL SELECT x, y FROM more_samples);
SELECT covar_samp(y, x) AS covariance FROM (SELECT x * 2 AS x, y * 2 AS y FROM samples);
WITH aggregated AS (SELECT AVG(x) AS avg_x, AVG(y) AS avg_y FROM samples) SELECT covar_samp(avg_y, avg_x) AS covariance FROM aggregated;
CREATE TABLE test1 (a INT, b FLOAT, c FLOAT);
CREATE TABLE test2 (d INT, e FLOAT, f FLOAT);
SELECT covar_samp(f, e) AS covariance FROM test1 JOIN test2 ON test1.a = test2.d;
CREATE TABLE empty_samples (id INT, x FLOAT, y FLOAT);
SELECT covar_samp(y, x) AS covariance FROM empty_samples;
SELECT covar_samp(y, x) AS covariance FROM samples GROUP BY id HAVING COUNT(*) > 1;
